items.title: DV attacker took photo of victim giving evidence in contempt of court jailed for more than four years
items.summary: A man who was found guilty of domestic violence offences and who pleaded guilty to contempt of court for photographing the victim giving evidence has been jailed for more than four years.

items.title: Iranian footballers backed by Afghan international cricketer Firooza Amiri to adjust to life in Australia
items.summary: Having fled Afghanistan as an 18-year-old, international cricketer Firooza Amiri knows what it is like to be a sports refugee from a war-torn country.

items.title: Sydney businessman Alexander Csergo found guilty of reckless foreign interference
items.summary: Australian businessman Alexander Csergo is found guilty of reckless foreign interference, after a Sydney court hears he compiled reports for two suspected Chinese spies while overseas.

items.title: Winter Paralympics coverage still sorely lacking, athletes and supporters say
items.summary: The Paralympic Winter Games are in full swing at Milano Cortina, and athletes and supporters have expressed their frustration over the lack of coverage of the event.

items.title: After almost 130 years in England, an Aboriginal man has been reburied on Country
items.summary: After almost 130 years, the remains of an Aboriginal man have made the long journey home from Oxford University to be reburied on the Country from where he was taken at Berowra Creek in Sydney.

items.title: What to watch, read and listen to this weekend: A new quiz show and a lovable goat
items.summary: This weekend try an unforgettable feta recipe or get lost in the hypnotic rhythms of legendary desert blues band Tinariwen's new album.

items.title: Flooding at Daly River community worst on record as Katherine mayor calls for Defence assistance
items.summary: Flooding in the remote Northern Territory town of Daly River has become the community's worst on record, surpassing the previous highest water level recorded in 1998.

items.title: Western Australia the big GST winner again, despite massive budget surpluses
items.summary: WA will get an extra $6.6 billion from taxpayers in the coming financial year under its special GST deal, despite being the only state with a budget surplus.

items.title: Parents in Alice Springs worry Birth and Beyond will close due to funding shortfall
items.summary: An "incredible" organisation delivering pregnancy and postnatal support in Alice Springs is calling for funding from both Northern Territory and federal governments to keep their doors open.

items.title: Cape York and Port Hedland flagged as Australia's highest risk entry points for lumpy skin disease
items.summary: Researchers have mapped the northern gateways where the cattle disease, lumpy skin disease, could first enter Australia, identifying Cape York Peninsula and Port Hedland as the highest-risk locations.

items.title: Former Taiwan men's coach kicked out of Women's Asian Cup match for 'political chants'
items.summary: The Women's Asian Cup has been embroiled in another diplomatic controversy, with Taiwanese officials lodging a complaint after a former national coach was ejected from a stadium for leading a chant which included the word "Taiwan."

items.title: Byron Bay's famous Bluesfest cancelled with less than three weeks' notice
items.summary: Thousands of music fans will be left scrambling to rearrange their Easter plans after one of Australia's last major festivals was called off with less than three weeks' notice.

items.title: Interruptions, campaign woes and 'razzle-dazzle': Here's how the SA leaders debate played out
items.summary: As South Australians get ready to vote in a week's time, Premier Peter Malinauskas and Opposition Leader Ashton Hurn went head-to-head in a campaign debate. Here's what they said.

items.title: Qantas COVID pandemic flight credits could be on their way to customers this year
items.summary: Qantas agrees to settle a class action ​related to flight credits issued during the COVID-19 ‌pandemic.

items.title: India continues to strengthen during ICC boss Jay Shah's tenure, but it may be coming at cricket's cost
items.summary: India releases a video of its celebrations following the T20 World Cup, and it features an unhealthy amount of a man who should not be there.

items.title: FIFA, AFC accused of failing their human rights obligations with Iranian women's footballers
items.summary: Pressure is growing on FIFA and the Asian Football Confederation to help the members of the Iranian women's football team returning home, and their families, and to learn lessons ahead of the men's World Cup.

items.title: Australia releases six days' worth of petrol and five of diesel from emergency stockpile
items.summary: Australia will make available about six days' worth of petrol from its emergency stockpile and five days of diesel, the first use of its fuel reserves since the invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

items.title: Jonah Pezet wants more after helping Parramatta Eels to upset NRL win over Brisbane Broncos
items.summary: Parramatta playmaker Jonah Pezet destroyed the team he will play for next year — but in a sign of his appetite for excellence, he was far from satisfied.

items.title: Oil storage, refineries and transport all in the firing line of Iran-US-Israeli war
items.summary: Drones and missiles have landed on key refineries and depots, destroying millions of barrels of oil production. ABC NEWS Verify maps key locations destroyed or targeted during the war.

items.title: NASA clears Artemis moon rocket for an April launch with four astronauts after repairs
items.summary: NASA has cleared its moon rocket for an April launch with four astronauts after completing the latest round of repairs.

items.title: Married at First Sight expert Mel Schilling reveals terminal cancer diagnosis
items.summary: MAFS expert Mel Schilling says she does not know how long she has left to live after revealing cancer has spread to her brain.

items.title: Researchers call for more awareness about the dangers of distracted driving
items.summary: Researchers warn eating, drinking and sudden loud noises are some of the most risky distractions for drivers, calling for more safety education.

items.title: How to remove mould from clothing and stop it growing in wardrobes and drawers
items.summary: Ever plan to wear your favourite jacket, only to pull it out of the wardrobe to discover it has a weird smell and is covered in mould? We explain how to remove it, and stop it from happening again.

items.title: Australia has never been more vulnerable to an energy crisis
items.summary: Australia is one of the biggest energy exporters on the planet, as the world's third-largest exporter of LNG and the biggest seaborne supplier of thermal and metallurgical coal. But when it comes to oil the country is horribly exposed.

items.title: Tips for how parents can talk to boys and young men about the manosphere
items.summary: Don't panic, don't erupt, remain open and ask questions: Here are some of the tips from experts on how to reclaim a loved one from the manosphere.

items.title: Police scour flooded Burnett River for missing Bundaberg man
items.summary: Emergency Services are searching the flooded Burnett River in Bundaberg for a man believed to be missing from a houseboat, and have airlifted a woman bitten by a snake from her isolated home.

items.title: Experts say pest control still possible as Australia-wide ban on commercial rat baits looms
items.summary: The federal chemical regulator has ruled that some of Australia's most popular over-the-counter rat baits will be taken off supermarket shelves, but experts say domestic and industry pest control is still viable.

items.title: Fremantle's window wide open in AFL season 2026, but more pain in store for West Coast
items.summary: The Fremantle Dockers and West Coast Eagles are starting the new AFL season at very different stages of the journey — and for one of them, the time to turn potential into performance and challenge for the premiership is now.

items.title: Teenage boy dies after allegedly stolen car crashes in Hoppers Crossing following carjacking attempt
items.summary: Police say the 15-year-old boy died when an allegedly stolen car carrying seven teenagers crashed at Hoppers Crossing on Thursday night.

items.title: Water Corporation's frustration with Alcoa over Perth's drinking water aired in internal documents
items.summary: Perth's water provider grew frustrated with US mining giant Alcoa after facing roadblocks in accessing areas near the city's key drinking water source that it needs to keep the supply safe, internal documents reveal.

items.title: NT authorities warn of crocodile risk as Top End flooding continues
items.summary: Crocodiles have been spotted lurking in floodwaters across the Top End, with NT Police incident controller Shaun Gill warning the reptiles are "absolutely everywhere" right now.

items.title: ASX follows Wall Street down as oil hits $US100 per barrel — as it happened
items.summary: The ASX 200 has finished the trading week in negative fashion after a mostly flat Friday the 13th. It followed the Dow Jones, which fell sharply overnight as the price of oil hit $US100 a barrel. Look back on the day's events and insights on the ABC News markets blog.

items.title: Donald Trump says Iran is welcome in US for FIFA World Cup, but believes they should not play
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says Iran men's national soccer team is welcome to participate in the 2026 World Cup, but believes it is not appropriate that they be there "for their own life and safety".

items.title: Sunraysia and Murray Valley farmers seek disaster declaration after deluge during harvest
items.summary: A deluge of rain at a critical stage of Sunraysia and Murray Valley's harvest and growing season leads farmers to call for it to be declared a disaster to unlock funds.

items.title: The Matildas played North Korea in Pyongyang in 2007. It was unlike anything they'd ever experienced before
items.summary: When the Matildas played North Korea in Pyongyang in 2007, it was like unlike anything they had experienced before.

items.title: Two teenagers die following e-bike and motorcycle crash south of Brisbane
items.summary: A 15-year-old girl and a 16-year-old boy have died after a motorcycle collided with the e-bike they were riding in Greenbank, south of Brisbane, on Thursday night.

items.title: Flood aftermath exposes holes in South Australia's federal road funding
items.summary: As floodwater recedes and local governments begin calculating the damage to SA roads, they are frustrated it will be up to them to foot the bill in lieu of federal government support.

items.title: Oscar Piastri moving on from Melbourne F1 crash and focused on Chinese Grand Prix
items.summary: Just days removed from one of the most crushing moments in his Formula 1 career, Oscar Piastri says he is only focused on the future.

items.summary: Hundreds of people in Queensland's Bundaberg region are cleaning up their homes and businesses as floodwaters recede, while residents in western Queensland prepare for flooding.

items.summary: NRL superstar Latrell Mitchell opens up about the Winmarra foundation, which he co-founded to empower emerging Indigenous leaders.

items.title: Liana France 15th in Milano Cortina Winter Paralympics Games women's giant slalom standing
items.summary: Flying a pink Australian flag with her face on it, para-alpine skier Liana France has marked herself as one to watch at Paralympics to come.

items.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas apologises for failing to deliver on his promise to 'fix the ramping crisis'
items.summary: South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas says there is "no shirking the fact" his government has failed to deliver on its key election promise to fix the ramping crisis — a failure for which he has apologised.

items.title: How Donald Trump and JD Vance are 'philosophically a little bit different' on Iran
items.summary: From MAGA-loathing "hillbilly" to vice-president, JD Vance's rise and rebirth have been something to behold. But the war in Iran may test his considerable political dexterity.

items.title: Stakeholders race to find Liberty Bell Bay smelter buyer as job losses loom
items.summary: A northern Tasmanian business group wants to secure a buyer for Australia's only manganese alloy smelter as the corporate regulator threatens its current owners with liquidation.

items.title: MP whose electorate gets largest capital gains tax discount wants to rein it in
items.summary: New analysis shows five of the six electorates with the largest capital gains tax discount benefits are held by independents.

items.title: On the Iraq-Iran border Kurdish troops are gathering to fight their own battle against the Khamenei regime
items.summary: On Iraq's border with Iran, troops are amassing as Iranian Kurds from across the globe gather to revolt after 47 years of being the victims of an oppressive and cruel regime.

items.title: What should you do with the family home after a divorce?
items.summary: For many Australians, the decision to divorce has become even more complicated because of the rising cost of housing. Some are now calling for policy changes to make it easier for single parents to re-enter the housing market.

items.title: Drought-hit WA pastoralists plead for funds to move hay stored 1,000km away
items.summary: After years of drought, pastoralists in WA are desperate for fodder for their cattle. A charity has a supply in storage for them — but no money for fuel to transport it.

items.title: 'Absolute madness': Broncos remain winless after Eels win bizarre NRL clash
items.summary: The Broncos' defence is labelled "pathetic" as they surrender a 20-6 lead, hit back in the second half but eventually lose 40-32 on the back of future teammate Jonah Pezet's game-sealing try.
